title: Performance issue with external sort
description:
### What happens?

I'm trying to run an external sort (see code). It quickly runs to 49% progress where it starts hanging for a long time, finishing after ~1.5 hours.

### To Reproduce

```
set threads=64;
SET memory_limit='1GB';
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE df AS (SELECT random() FROM range(1_000_000_000));
SELECT * FROM df ORDER BY "random()";
```

### OS:

Debian Linux 12 x86_64

### DuckDB Version:

v1.1.4-dev3741 ab8c909857

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

Dual AMD EPYC 7742 64-Core Processor, 2 TB RAM

### Full Name:

Benjamin Buchfink

### Affiliation:

Max Planck Institute for Biology Tübingen

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a source build

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value DOUBLE, name VARCHAR(50), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value, name) VALUES (1, DBMS_RANDOM.VALUE(), 'test1'), (2, DBMS_RANDOM.VALUE(), 'test2'), (3, DBMS_RANDOM.VALUE(), NULL);
CREATE TABLE additional_data (ref_id INTEGER, description TEXT, FOREIGN KEY (ref_id) REFERENCES test_data(id));
SELECT t.id, t.value, a.description FROM test_data t LEFT JOIN additional_data a ON t.id = a.ref_id ORDER BY t.value DESC;
